segments.text: And he commanded the steward of his house, saying,
Fill the men's sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put every man's money in his sack's mouth.
segments.speaker: Joseph

footnotes: the steward…: Heb. him that was over his house

segments.text: And he put my cup, the silver cup, in the sack's mouth of the youngest, and his corn money. And he did according to the word that Joseph had spoken.
As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away, they and their asses.
And when they were gone out of the city, and not yet far off,
Up, follow after the men; and when thou dost overtake them, say unto them, Wherefore have ye rewarded evil for good?
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Is not this it in which my lord drinketh, and whereby indeed he divineth? ye have done evil in so doing.
segments.speaker: Joseph's Steward

segments.text: And he overtook them, and he spake unto them these same words.
Wherefore saith my lord these words? God forbid that thy servants should do according to this thing:
segments.speaker: Joseph's Brothers

segments.text: Behold, the money, which we found in our sacks' mouths, we brought again unto thee out of the land of Canaan: how then should we steal out of thy lord's house silver or gold?
segments.speaker: Joseph's Brothers

segments.text: With whomsoever of thy servants it be found, both let him die, and we also will be my lord's bondmen.
segments.speaker: Joseph's Brothers

segments.text: Now also let it be according unto your words: he with whom it is found shall be my servant; and ye shall be blameless.
segments.speaker: Joseph's Steward

segments.text: Then they speedily took down every man his sack to the ground, and opened every man his sack.
And he searched, and began at the eldest, and left at the youngest: and the cup was found in Benjamin's sack.
Then they rent their clothes, and laded every man his ass, and returned to the city.
And Judah and his brethren came to Joseph's house; for he was yet there: and they fell before him on the ground.
What deed is this that ye have done? wot ye not that such a man as I can certainly divine?
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: What shall we say unto my lord? what shall we speak? or how shall we clear ourselves? God hath found out the iniquity of thy servants: behold, we are my lord's servants, both we, and he also with whom the cup is found.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: God forbid that I should do so: but the man in whose hand the cup is found, he shall be my servant; and as for you, get you up in peace unto your father.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Oh my lord, let thy servant, I pray thee, speak a word in my lord's ears, and let not thine anger burn against thy servant: for thou art even as Pharaoh.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: My lord asked his servants, saying, Have ye a father, or a brother?
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: We have a father, an old man, and a child of his old age, a little one; and his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother, and his father loveth him.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And thou saidst unto thy servants, Bring him down unto me, that I may set mine eyes upon him.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And we said unto my lord, The lad cannot leave his father: for if he should leave his father, his father would die.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And thou saidst unto thy servants, Except your youngest brother come down with you, ye shall see my face no more.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And it came to pass when we came up unto thy servant my father, we told him the words of my lord.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And our father said, Go again, and buy us a little food.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And we said, We cannot go down: if our youngest brother be with us, then will we go down: for we may not see the man's face, except our youngest brother be with us.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And thy servant my father said unto us, Ye know that my wife bare me two sons:
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And the one went out from me, and I said, Surely he is torn in pieces; and I saw him not since:
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And if ye take this also from me, and mischief befall him, ye shall bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: Now therefore when I come to thy servant my father, and the lad be not with us; seeing that his life is bound up in the lad's life;
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: It shall come to pass, when he seeth that the lad is not with us, that he will die: and thy servants shall bring down the gray hairs of thy servant our father with sorrow to the grave.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: For thy servant became surety for the lad unto my father, saying, If I bring him not unto thee, then I shall bear the blame to my father for ever.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: Now therefore, I pray thee, let thy servant abide instead of the lad a bondman to my lord; and let the lad go up with his brethren.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: For how shall I go up to my father, and the lad be not with me? lest peradventure I see the evil that shall come on my father.
segments.speaker: Judah
